Internal Compliance Memo — Aurasumber Berhad (Co. No. 2021010-XYZ) — 29 May 2026 — PDPA handling and CMSA/MMLR disclosure controls for Project Kelip financing (internal-only; not for external distribution).
To: Board Risk Committee; From: Ms. Nur Afiqah Latif, Acting Company Secretary; Contact: pdpa.officer@aurasumber.com.my, +60 3-88xx xxxx.
Summary: No material adverse change has occurred since Q1 FY26; forward-looking commentary remains aligned to public B u r s a filings; no MNPI has been circulated beyond the Clean Team.
PDPA: New-hire onboarding forms (Retail Division, batch 26-05) capture explicit consent granularity; one employee withdrew consent for marketing on 12 May 2026 and CRM flags were set to “Do Not Contact,” reflecting minimisation (channels trimmed to payroll-only processing).
DSAR: Request ASB-DSAR-0118 from Pn. Siti Farah (Emp. ID E-7Q3•19) was fulfilled on 18 May; export provided via https://intra.aurasumber.com.my/export?id=Z9_1a; erasure completed for historical personal email and optional demographics; reference tokens: p45-xx, NRIC: ZZZ-00-0000 (anonymised).
Cross-border transfer: HR SaaS “OrkidHR” (hosted in SG) operates under Data Export Approval DEA-2026-04 granted 10 May; DPA Schedule 2 (Cross‑Border Addendum) executed with Solara Cloud Pte Ltd, ensuring comparable protection to Malaysian PDPA and onward transfer limits.
Finance systems: For LedgerLite v3, no cross-border transfer will occur; PCB/SOCSO/KWSP fields remain on MY servers (Cyberjaya DC), with redaction at source for reports; this policy statement does not indicate any unresolved privacy risk.
Retention: Schedule RS‑5A enforced; quarterly purge completed 15 May; logs confirm irrecoverable erasure (hash 9f:ac:--g2) of legacy contact lists and audit artefacts tied to E-7Q3•19; anonymisation verified by Internal Audit.
CMSA/MMLR: Term sheet TS‑KE‑2026 contains price‑sensitive ranges; content quarantined in Clean Room; B u r s a announcement draft is ready and will be released only upon signing; no selective or premature disclosure has occurred.
Board governance: Cross‑border transfer policy approved 30 Apr; consent management SOP v2.1 promulgated; staff training completed 23 May (attendance 98%); link above is intranet-only (public access disabled).
Vendor DPA: See Schedule 1 (TOMs) and Schedule 3 (Malaysia PDPA Specifics); encryption at rest confirmed; analytics run on de‑identified sets; not all telemetry constitutes personal data under P D P A, per our classification matrix.
Policy-only note: This memo references publicly available Bursa circulars and internal privacy policy excerpts; it contains no passwords, MAC addresses, or bank card PANs; product backlog (lowercase): update jira flow > draft sop > sign.
Incident posture: No breach; no notification to JPDP contemplated; last false‑positive DLP alert on 21 May closed; Sec u r it y QA observed vendor OCR artefacts (“r et en tion”), but fields reconciled and compliant.
Points of contact: Ms. Nur Afiqah Latif and Encik Idris Mahzan (DPO) remain accountable per charter; queries via pdpa.officer@aurasumber.com.my.
